headline: Iranian Guard Strikes Vessel as Israel Pulls Back from Southern Lebanon
subheadline: Revolutionary Guard damaged a Singapore‑flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz; Israeli troops retreat, leaving a Golani vehicle crash killing soldier Basel.
lede: The Iranian Revolutionary Guard damaged a Singapore‑flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, causing no casualties. Israeli forces are pulling back from positions in southern Lebanon, creating a potential vacuum for Lebanese armed groups. A Golani brigade vehicle overturned in the same area, killing a soldier named Basel. Simultaneously, Ukraine forced Russian occupiers off the Kinburn Spit while Russia launched missile and drone strikes on civilian targets in Sumy, Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv.

sections.title: Ukraine Reclaims Kinburn Spit, Russia Strikes Back
sections.body: Ukrainian Southern Defense Forces delivered heavy artillery fire on the Kinburn Spit, forcing Russian occupiers to withdraw and evacuate survivors. Russia responded with an Iskander‑M ballistic missile and a swarm of 90 drones, injuring civilians in Sumy and damaging a gas station in Zaporizhzhia. Additional drone attacks wounded five civilians in Bogodukhov, Kharkiv region, and ignited a garage and three cars. The Russian‑controlled oil depot "Poltavskaya" in Kuban was hit again, with at least three fuel tanks burning.

sections.title: Iran Attacks Vessel, Israel Withdraws from Lebanon
sections.body: Revolutionary Guard units struck a Singapore‑flagged cargo ship near Oman in the Strait of Hormuz, inflicting damage but no casualties. Israeli forces are reported to be pulling back from southern Lebanon, opening the area to possible occupation by Lebanese armed groups. A Golani brigade vehicle overturned in southern Lebanon, resulting in the death of a soldier named Basel. Gulf states have agreed to bar Iran from collecting fees in the Strait, while Iran rejected a UN‑backed proposal to free detained ships, heightening maritime tension.

sections.title: Intelligence Assessment
sections.body: The Guard's maritime attack underscores Tehran's willingness to threaten critical chokepoints, risking global oil flow disruptions. Israel's withdrawal creates a security vacuum that Lebanese militias may exploit, raising the prospect of renewed cross‑border clashes.
